title: Define and enforce tool-call schemas
description: Tool/function definitions exposed to the model have strict argument schemas, and returned arguments are validated before any tool executes.
body: Use when exposing functions/tools the model can invoke. Do not pass model-produced arguments straight into a tool, and avoid loose schemas that accept free-form strings where an enum or id belongs. Actions: declare each tool with typed, constrained parameters; validate the returned arguments against that schema; reject or repair out-of-range values before execution; gate side-effecting tools behind permission checks. Validation signals include tests where the model returns malformed or out-of-range arguments and the executor refuses. Risk boundary: a side-effecting tool (write, delete, external call) invoked with unvalidated arguments is a critical finding requiring a human gate.
